title: Elon Musk loses lawsuit against Sam Altman and OpenAI
summary: A jury ruled that Elon Musk's lawsuit against Sam Altman and OpenAI was dismissed because it was filed too late, specifically finding that Musk could have brought claims as early as 2019 or 2021 regarding similar Microsoft deals. This ruling sets a precedent regarding the statute of limitations for challenging non-profit to for-profit transitions of AI entities, potentially affecting future governance disputes in the AI industry. The jury answered only yes/no questions, so the exact reasoning is unknown, but the ruling suggests that the 2023 Microsoft deal central to the lawsuit was too similar to earlier deals, making the claims untimely.
context: Musk co-founded OpenAI as a non-profit in 2015, but left in 2018. OpenAI later restructured into a for-profit entity and formed partnerships with Microsoft. Musk sued in 2023, alleging breach of contract and fiduciary duty. The case was dismissed on statute of limitations grounds.
discussion: Commenters generally agree that the legal outcome was based on timeliness, but some feel there should still be consequences for OpenAI's structure. There is discussion about the precedent for non-profit to for-profit transfers and the grounds for appeal.
